steps.title: Use a scalar-multiple shortcut
steps.content: A linear system has infinitely many solutions when the two equations are the same line. That means every coefficient in one equation is the same multiple of the matching coefficient in the other equation.

steps.title: Let Desmos solve the multiplier
steps.content: You can make Desmos compare the $x$- and $y$-coefficients at once. Enter two helper lists, then regress the second equation's coefficients against a multiple $s$ of the first equation's coefficients. Desmos returns $s=\frac{10}{13}$.

steps.title: Convert the multiplier to the requested ratio
steps.content: The same multiplier applies to the constants, so $k=s g$. The question asks for $\frac{g}{k}$, which is the reciprocal of $s$: $$\frac{g}{k}=\frac{1}{10/13}=\frac{13}{10}=1.3$$ The answer is 1.3,13/10.
